title: Reflection
text: Creating this ePortfolio has been one of the most valuable learning experiences in this module, as it required me to combine technical skills, organisation, creativity, and critical self-reflection.

title: Reflection
text:
What I Learned
Through this project, I developed practical skills in Quarto, Git, GitHub, Python, and web design. I learned how to structure a professional website, organise files effectively, manage changes using version control, and present work in a clear and visually engaging way.

title: Reflection
text:
Challenges I Faced
One of the main challenges was understanding how all the technical components of the portfolio worked together. I had to learn how Quarto uses file structure, page rendering, and YAML configuration to control navigation and layout.
I also encountered issues with broken links, page structure, Python package setup, and rendering project pages correctly in Quarto.

title: Reflection
text:
How I Solved Problems
I approached these challenges by working through them step by step. I tested changes, corrected file paths, improved my Quarto configuration, and installed the required Python packages.
This process showed me that technical work often involves debugging, testing, and refining solutions, rather than expecting everything to work immediately.






Important



A key lesson from this project is that professional digital work is not only about writing content — it also involves testing, debugging, refining, and presenting work effectively.

title: Reflection
text:
Skills Development
This project strengthened my confidence in: - building and styling a website using Quarto
- managing a project using Git and GitHub
- using Python for data analysis and visualisation
- presenting academic work in a clear and professional format

title: Reflection
text:
Challenge Example
One specific challenge I faced was configuring Python within Quarto. Missing dependencies caused errors during rendering, which prevented my project pages from working correctly.
I resolved this by installing the required packages and ensuring the Python environment was correctly configured. This improved my troubleshooting ability and gave me a better understanding of how development environments function.

title: Reflection
text:
Future Application
The skills developed in this project will be valuable in both academic and professional contexts. In the future, I aim to build more advanced data analysis projects and continue improving how I communicate technical work.
This experience has increased my confidence in using digital tools not only to complete assignments, but also to create work that is polished, purposeful, and professionally presented.

title: Student Performance Analysis
text: This project analyses the relationship between study hours and student performance using Python. It demonstrates how data can be organised, visualised, and interpreted to identify trends.

title: Student Performance Analysis
text: This project analyses the relationship between study hours and student performance using Python. It demonstrates how data can be organised, visualised, and interpreted to identify trends.

title: Student Performance Analysis
text:
Objectives

Organise data in a clear and structured format

Analyse the relationship between study time and performance

Visualise results using Python

Interpret findings and draw conclusions

title: Student Performance Analysis
text:
Python Code


Code
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

df = pd.DataFrame({
    "StudyHours": [1, 2, 3, 4, 5],
    "Score": [50, 55, 65, 72, 85]
})

df

fig = px.line(
    df,
    x="StudyHours",
    y="Score",
    markers=True,
    title="Study Hours vs Score"
)

fig

title: Student Performance Analysis
text:
Interpretation
The chart shows a clear positive relationship between study hours and student performance. As study time increases, scores also increase, indicating a strong upward trend.
This suggests that increased study time has a direct impact on academic performance in this dataset.






TipKey Insight



The analysis indicates a clear positive relationship between study hours and academic performance. This suggests that consistent study time can have a meaningful effect on academic outcomes.

title: Student Performance Analysis
text:
Reflection
This project demonstrates how data analysis can be used to identify relationships between variables.
Through this task, I developed my ability to create datasets, generate visualisations, and interpret trends. In future, this analysis could be extended by including more data points or additional variables such as revision methods or subject difficulty.
This project demonstrates my ability to apply Python, data analysis, and data visualisation techniques to identify and communicate meaningful insights.

title: Monthly Budget Analysis
text: This project analyses a simple monthly budget using Python. It demonstrates how financial data can be organised and visualised to better understand spending patterns and support decision-making.

title: Monthly Budget Analysis
text: This project analyses a simple monthly budget using Python. It demonstrates how financial data can be organised and visualised to better understand spending patterns and support decision-making.

title: Monthly Budget Analysis
text:
Objective
The objective of this project is to examine how expenses are distributed across different categories and identify areas of high spending.

title: Monthly Budget Analysis
text:
Python Code


Code
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

budget = pd.DataFrame({
    "Category": ["Rent", "Food", "Transport", "Entertainment", "Savings"],
    "Amount": [800, 250, 100, 120, 300]
})

budget

fig = px.pie(
    budget,
    values="Amount",
    names="Category",
    title="Monthly Budget Distribution",
    hole=0.3
)

fig
